events.title: US Launches Fresh Strikes on Iran
events.desc: The United States conducted a new wave of strikes against Iran on Wednesday night, with reported explosions in Bandar Abbas, Chabahar, and Ahvaz. The US stated these actions are aimed at forcing open the Strait of Hormuz and weakening Iran's military capabilities for potential complex operations. This escalates the ongoing conflict between the two nations.

events.title: Iran Says Peace Deal Voided Amid US Attacks
events.desc: Following a new wave of US strikes, Iran declared its peace deal with the United States void. The Iranian government stated it is fighting an 'existential war,' indicating a significant escalation in the conflict. This development signals a breakdown in diplomatic efforts and a deepening of hostilities.

events.title: US Strikes Target Iranian Military Capabilities
events.desc: Recent US strikes on Iran are reportedly designed to weaken specific Iranian military capabilities, potentially in preparation for more complex operations. Officials indicate these strikes aim to open the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting a multi-faceted strategic objective beyond immediate naval passage.

events.title: Trump Threatens 'Pickaxe Mountain' Nuclear Site
events.desc: President Donald Trump has threatened to attack 'Pickaxe Mountain,' a fortified underground site linked to Iran's nuclear program. This direct threat highlights the critical focus on preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons and signifies a severe escalation in rhetoric and potential action.

events.title: Ships Refuse US-Guided Hormuz Transits After Attacks
events.desc: Several shipping companies are avoiding U.S. military-guided transit schemes through the Strait of Hormuz due to safety concerns following a series of Iranian attacks. This indicates a growing reluctance among commercial vessels to operate in the volatile waterway, impacting global trade.

events.title: Lebanon, Israel Conclude Talks on 'Pilot Zones'
events.desc: Lebanon and Israel concluded U.S.-brokered talks in Rome, making progress on a plan that could lead to Israeli forces withdrawing from parts of southern Lebanon. This development comes amidst the ongoing regional conflict and represents a diplomatic effort to de-escalate tensions along the Israeli-Lebanese border.

events.title: US Sanctions Disrupt Iran's Weapon Procurement
events.desc: The U.S. government has imposed sanctions targeting individuals and entities involved in an international network facilitating Iran's weapon procurement. The sanctioned parties include Iranian and Russian nationals, as well as entities based in Iran, Russia, and Nigeria, aiming to hinder Iran's access to weaponry.

events.title: US House Defeats Amendment to Cut Off Israel Aid
events.desc: The U.S. House of Representatives narrowly defeated an amendment that sought to cut off aid to Israel. The vote, which saw significant support from Democrats, highlights a growing divide within the party regarding U.S. policy towards Israel and its actions in Gaza.

events.title: Yemen's Houthis Launch Missiles at Saudi Arabia After Sanaa Strikes
events.desc: The Iran-backed Houthi group launched missiles targeting Saudi Arabia, specifically Abha airport, in retaliation for reported strikes on Sanaa airport. This action signifies a continuation of the conflict in Yemen and retaliatory actions between the involved parties.

events.title: UK Bans Support for Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps
events.desc: The United Kingdom has officially banned support for Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC). This move, utilizing new powers, targets an organization linked to death threats and intimidation on British soil, signaling a hardening stance against Iran's security apparatus.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Attacks as Khamenei is Buried
events.desc: The US and Iran engaged in a significant exchange of strikes, with Centcom reporting hits on 90 Iranian targets. This occurred during the period of public mourning for the recently deceased Ayatollah Khamenei, indicating that regional tensions remain high despite internal events in Iran.

events.title: Israeli Strike on Gaza Police Post Kills Seven
events.desc: An Israeli strike on a police post in northern Gaza resulted in the deaths of seven individuals, including a senior officer in the Hamas-run police force. Israel's military stated the targets were 'terrorists,' underscoring the ongoing kinetic exchanges in the region.

events.title: AI Tool Exploited to Sexualize Minors, Lawsuit Filed
events.desc: AI company xAI has filed a lawsuit against a user accused of exploiting its AI tools to create explicit deepfakes involving minors. This highlights the growing concerns around the misuse of AI technology and the challenges in preventing the creation of harmful content.

events.title: Google Sued Over AI Copyright Infringement
events.desc: Authors and publishers, led by Hachette and Elsevier, have filed a lawsuit against Google, alleging copyright infringement through the use of their books to train Google's Gemini AI model. This case raises significant questions about intellectual property rights in the age of AI development.

events.title: Houthi Threat to Maritime Shipping Re-evaluated
events.desc: Two years after initial analysis, the Houthi threat to maritime shipping, particularly in the Red Sea and Strait of Hormuz, remains a significant concern. While attention has shifted, the underlying issues of navigation freedom and regional stability persist, requiring ongoing monitoring and potential reassessment of strategies.

infrastructure.name: Bandar Abbas
infrastructure.note: Key Iranian port and naval base

infrastructure.name: Chabahar
infrastructure.note: Important Iranian port on the Gulf of Oman

infrastructure.name: Ahvaz
infrastructure.note: City in southwestern Iran, near the Iraqi border
